title: Prior session's prepared dispatch script carried stale premises across the session boundary; the audit it was built to address had already passed
content: Prior session ended with a hand-prepared managed-agent dispatch script (pasted into this session as a plain-text artifact). The script's task prompt was built around 'Read and address the canon-code-sync-audit failure' on PR #69, with elaborate fix proposals (add implementation_status to D0030 frontmatter, investigate supersession-chain integrity around extended_by, etc.) and a 7-step gauntlet checklist still showing as in-progress. Reality check before executing the script revealed: the canon-code-sync-audit ran four cycles against PR #69. Cycles 1-3 (against intermediate SHAs 8a72202b and earlier) returned FAIL. Cycle 4, against the current branch tip 52d7f163, returned PASS at 2026-05-08T16:00:12Z (issue comment 4407849028). All three blocking gates (Workers Builds × 2, canon-code sync audit) reported success. The PASS verdict explicitly endorsed the canon-only scoping ('canonical prescription preceding implementation — the ODD pattern — not drift') and classified the two informational notes (How-to-Join text ahead of code; SessionDO vs AmsMcpAgent naming) as non-blocking and scoped to the implementation PR. The script's premises were frozen at prior-session-end-state, before the final audit cycle completed. Verified by GET /repos/.../pulls/69 and GET /repos/.../commits/52d7f163.../check-runs.

title: A hand-prepared script handed across a session boundary is itself a context-carry vehicle, even when the new session is nominally 'fresh'
content: klappy://canon/methods/fresh-session-over-context-carry argues that context staleness compounds and canon does not, so phase boundaries should default to opening a fresh session that reads the merged contract from canon. The method's anti-pattern 'I have all the context loaded, why re-fetch?' is what the prior session committed by preparing a fully-elaborated dispatch script: the script was an in-context-resident contract, not a canon-resident one. The fresh session pattern was physically observed (this session is fresh; the prior session's chat UI died) but its substantive purpose was defeated by the script smuggling the prior session's frozen mental model across the break. Extension of the parent canon: a prepared script (or briefing, or 'here's exactly what to do' artifact) IS context-carry, and inherits whatever staleness existed at prior-session-end-state. The cure is the same as the parent canon prescribes: re-read canon and current reality at start-of-session; treat any inherited script as a hint about prior intent, not an authoritative directive. This worked example also strengthens the parent canon's third anti-pattern: 'It's faster to continue' — applied here, 'it's faster to just run the script' — was usually-true-for-the-next-ten-minutes, usually-false-for-the-rest-of-the-day. Running the script as-written would have either dispatched a managed agent on a wild goose chase or produced spurious 'fix' commits chasing a phantom drift. Verifying state first cost two minutes and reduced cleanup to two GitHub API calls.

title: Verify reality first; do the trivial cleanup directly rather than dispatch a managed agent against a phantom failure
content: Two paths were available: (a) execute the prepared dispatch script as-written, sending a managed agent to fix an audit failure; or (b) verify the audit state first, then choose the right tool for whatever actually remained. Chose (b). Justification: (1) Foundational Axiom 1 (Reality Is Sovereign) — observe before asserting; (2) Foundational Axiom 4 (You Cannot Verify What You Did Not Observe) — the prior agent's 'audit failed' framing was unverified by this session; (3) bottleneck-respect canon — minimize operator wait time; an agent dispatch + 5-15 minute polling loop versus two HTTP calls; (4) the dispatch script's hard-coded fix proposals were specific enough that a fresh agent might apply them anyway, producing spurious commits. Outcome: verification surfaced PASS; cleanup was REST PATCH (body refresh) + GraphQL markPullRequestReadyForReview (draft → ready). Total active session time from script-paste to closure: ~4 minutes. PR #69 closed: state=open, draft=false, body refreshed with 8/8 gauntlet items green and audit-response section added.

title: When inheriting a prepared plan or script across a session boundary, MUST re-verify its load-bearing premises against current canon and current reality before executing; MUST NOT execute solely on the basis of the prepared plan's authority
content: MUST: At session start, when receiving a prepared script, briefing, or directive from a prior session, identify each load-bearing premise in the artifact (e.g., 'audit X is failing', 'commit Y is the latest', 'feature Z is unimplemented'). For each premise, perform a direct observation against the current state (repo state via API, canon via oddkit_get/oddkit_resolve, deployment state via live verification). Only after premise-verification proceed to execute. MUST NOT: Treat a prepared script's authority as a substitute for direct observation. The prior session's mental model was frozen at prior-session-end-state; anything that has changed since (CI runs, audit verdicts, merges, deployments, canon updates) is invisible to the prepared artifact. MUST NOT: Skip verification because the script is detailed or seemingly self-contained. Detail does not equal currency. Rationale: this is a worked-example extension of klappy://canon/methods/fresh-session-over-context-carry. The parent canon names the failure mode for in-session context-staleness; this constraint extends the same discipline to cross-session-handoff artifacts. A prepared script is context-carry by another name. Promotion question (open): whether this constraint warrants a sibling canon entry on klappy.dev (e.g., klappy://canon/constraints/handoff-artifacts-require-premise-verification) or whether the parent method's anti-pattern list adequately covers it. Decision deferred to operator at promotion time.

title: Open question for next operator-review cycle: promote this observation to klappy.dev as a sibling under fresh-session-over-context-carry, or leave as AMS journal entry
content: This observation is currently captured as an AMS-repo journal entry (this file). The substantive lesson generalizes beyond AMS — it concerns cross-session handoff hygiene, which is a klappy.dev-tier concern. Two options: (1) leave as AMS journal entry only; the parent canon (klappy://canon/methods/fresh-session-over-context-carry) implicitly covers it via the 'I have all the context loaded' anti-pattern; (2) promote to klappy.dev as a new constraint or as an extension of the parent method's anti-pattern list, with this entry referenced as the worked example. Recommendation: defer the promotion question to a future klappy.dev session that's already touching session-discipline canon (avoid drive-by additions). For now, this entry is the durable observation surface and any agent reading klappy://canon/methods/fresh-session-over-context-carry should follow the cross-link back to this journal for the worked example. Cross-references for the next session to consider: klappy://canon/principles/verification-requires-fresh-context (related but distinct — that one is about author/critic separation; this one is about premise-currency in handoffs).
